Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! Now I see only g (gray) cells at (15, 31) and (51, 43), and there's a purple/magenta object in the bottom-left corner. The green cells have disappeared and been replaced by gray cells. This is different from level 1.
Looking at the image, there's a purple/magenta square with a white center in the bottom-left corner. This seems to be a new element. Let me check all the nodes more carefully.
Wait - looking at the image more carefully, I see:
- Top panel: all gray cells, one gray cell where green was
- Bottom-right panel: one gray cell where green was  
- Bottom-left: a purple/magenta square with white center
It seems like the green cells have been "used up" and turned gray. But there are only 2 g nodes showing. The level isn't complete yet. Let me check what other colored objects exist.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: Level 2 - green cells turn gray after being moved. A purple/magenta object appeared in the bottom-left. Need to investigate this new object and figure out the completion condition.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check all colored nodes on the board
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] not in ('W', 'w', 'S'):  # Skip background
        boundary = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in boundary)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in boundary)
        max_r = max(p[0] for p in boundary)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in boundary)
        print(f"  {n['color']}: top-left=({min_r},{min_c}) size=({max_r-min_r+1}x{max_c-min_c+1}) pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Try clicking the purple object
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 56, 'col': 6}])

seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
print(f"\nAfter clicking purple object:")
for n in seg2['nodes']:
    if n['color'] not in ('W', 'w', 'S'):
        boundary = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in boundary)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in boundary)
        max_r = max(p[0] for p in boundary)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in boundary)
        print(f"  {n['color']}: top-left=({min_r},{min_c}) size=({max_r-min_r+1}x{max_c-min_c+1})")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
